mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single monthly release, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 4.5% in a single monthly release
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the threshold crossing fails to predict a drop in forward returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in private nonfarm payrolls indicates a synchronized supply-demand mismatch that erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and PAYEMS does not predict negative 60-day forward returns more often than chance, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a coefficient with p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate environment, a sustained increase in housing starts acts as a leading indicator of resilient consumer demand and construction sector health, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to correlate with positive 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or the conditional forward return differential drops below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPI index exceeding 0.4% signals accelerating inflationary pressures that force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPI spikes above the threshold do not lead to negative 5-day forward returns in more than 40% of cases, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS levels.

mechanism_narrative: When private payrolls grow robustly while the unemployment rate simultaneously falls, it confirms a self-reinforcing labor demand expansion that supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE does not predict positive 60-day forward returns with a hit rate above 55%, or the interaction effect disappears in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP directly reflects expanding aggregate demand, which translates into higher corporate earnings per share and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth quarters consistently fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns, or the rolling correlation between GDP growth and SPX returns drops below 0.10 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in the industrial production index below 98.0 indicates a severe supply-side bottleneck or demand collapse that disrupts corporate operations, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: supply chain stress or demand shock
failure_condition: INDPRO dropping below 98.0 does not predict negative 20-day forward returns with a statistical significance of p < 0.05, or the signal generates false positives in >50% of historical instances.
